item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand that the character's behaviour was being affected by the anomaly, but I just couldn't stop thinking about the cliched way characters in horror movies act stupid. It doesn't matter if a character is stupid through bad writing or through anomalous effects, if I they don't act like humans would act, how can I put myself in their shoes?

The behavioural changes weren't enough to make the character's actions interesting or creepy, and just felt like an excuse to have him act stupid. SCP-2678 also had the anomaly affect the character's behaviour, but it was done very creepily and actually felt more than just not caring about stuff. If an anomaly affects someone's personality, it should do more than just feel like a contrived way to make the character not care about the situation.

-1

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: I'm almost certain that 'viscous' would fit context better than 'vicious'.

item.title: Whoopsie
item.content:encoded: Fixed (changed the collapsible to 1). I must have messed that up when I changed it to be a collapsible, thanks for letting me know :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there's a reference to "Proposal 4691-1" which doesn't exist, might want to change that to -A?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this wholeheartedly, the last collapsible is basically unnecessary and detracts from the rest of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't like it.
Suburban areas is too vague of a location and if the skip occurs in that environment it's very likely that the victim can go for months without rescue.
Also only the one instance is noted, which is the hook. Creepy enough but it's just a tale in an scp article wrapper. A science document would have more about the dates, locations, victim profiles, durations or something concrete to assist with discovery rescue containment cleanup.
The end is a real turnoff, even if there's something to learn here the dr is suggesting to use innocent civilians for testing, not trained staff or D class. That's rather worse than lolfoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This does a lot right and I enjoyed reading through it, however I didn't find the ending very relevant or satisfying. If the article ended before the final collapsible I think it would've been better.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I'm glad you like it. I'll polish up these bits, I'm never sure how they slip through the cracks.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Here be Crit/Opinion
byebye

Okay LittleFieryOne, let's see what you got.

Nitty-Gritty:

are to be x2

The first and third sentences of your con procs have "are to be". You might want to change one of them.

Only one resident resides within the location.

You should put "occupies" (or something like it) instead of having resident resides which seems a bit redundant.

The maximum duration of an SCP-4691 event is potentially limitless.

This sentence feels off to me, I think that paragraph could be reworked to have this at the end.

In all known instances, the resident within attracts the attention of citizens on the outside.

Cut "within"

though evidence of this has yet to surface.

This should be reworded to sound a bit more clinical.

…though it is unlikely that they would continue for an extended period of without being discovered.

cut "of" or add "time" after

I creeped down the stairs.

creeped -> crept

I eventually prodded one of the sunny-side up eggs. Warm, soft. Tempting, but I still chucked it.

"sunny-side-up eggs"

it was logically when someone would serve a lunch.

Cut "a" before lunch.

I shut them tight, and hid back around the corner.

Cut the comma here, in the notes there are a lot of unneeded commas (just something to look out for).

..had been consuming a thick mixture of mucus and dust in amounts..

(1:) EW (2:) maybe use the word "vicious" instead of thick.

Proposal 4691-A

To be honest I think this note could be better, as the final bit of the article you really want this part to shine.

Opinion: This is a good piece and a thoroughly creepy anomaly, nice job! I do however think there is room for improvement of tone and execution, specifically in the conprocs/notes/final notice. That said you have demonstrated willingness to improve your article in the past and hopefully will continue to do so with this one. With that in mind and the fact the I genuinely enjoyed the piece, this was an easy +1 for me.

Good Job and best of luck!!



Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to DrAkimoto or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: Re: Five Star Stay-cation
item.content:encoded: I like this, its the first SCP i've read in a while that I found actually kinda spooky. I applaud you from keeping away from compulsion to make them eat the food or not try the doors or whatever, I like how it's all just logical steps that lead to illogical behaviour. +1

item.title: Five Star Stay-cation
item.content:encoded: I found this one really interesting. I guess it qualifies as a cognitohazard since it manifested food in the mind of the victim when really he was consuming the goop. I guess I can relate to this, however, since this is practically how I live my life as it is.

item.title: Re: Yikes
item.content:encoded: If I read correctly, he just stayed there for about 2 weeks, tho

item.title: Yikes
item.content:encoded: Damn, this guy consumed mucus and dust in his room for years? That sends me some deep shivers. Id like to know where he got it. +1

item.title: Turns out, I couldn't come up with a better title
item.content:encoded:
I would like to thank cybersqyd, UraniumEmpire, RockTeethMothEyes (especially for suggesting the better ending), Pedantique, Calibri Bold, Dyslexion, Levi, and ImmortalPhoenix43 for taking the time to provide feedback and for all the advice.

I hope people like this one. But if this one goes down, it goes down. You know. Eh.

EDIT: This is my first article to get +9, so I'm kind of ecstatic right now :)
